_meta.description: Radiant gains section registered input nodes. Uses a row-based pattern (rows 73-78) where each row represents a fenestration group (Doors, Window N/E/S/W, Skylight). Each row has 5 inputs: area, orientation, SHGC, winter shading %, summer shading %. Plus a utilization method selector. Total 31 inputs (6 rows x 5 fields + 1 method).

fieldsPerRow.label: {LABEL} Area
fieldsPerRow.note: Fenestration area. Bridged TO TransmissionLoss as computed nodes (d_88-d_93).

fieldsPerRow.label: {LABEL} Orientation
fieldsPerRow.note: Compass orientation for solar radiation lookup.

fieldsPerRow.label: {LABEL} Winter Shading
fieldsPerRow.note: Winter season shading percentage (0=no shading, 100=fully shaded).

standalone.label: Gains Utilization Method
standalone.note: Method for calculating solar gains utilization factor. Options include fixed percentages and dynamic methods.

signature.inputs: These ARE the inputs (registered via graph.registerInput in RadiantGainsNodes.js, generated by loop over rows 73-78)
signature.output: Values stored in MultiModelState, consumed by solar gain calculations (S10), and bridged to TransmissionLoss areas (S11)

preconditions.description: Solar heat gain coefficient is a ratio

invariants.description: All radiant gain inputs can differ between Target and Reference models

invariants.condition: Row areas bridge to TransmissionLoss fenestration areas

behavior.output: Triggers recomputation of solar gains for that row AND bridged TransmissionLoss area node
behavior.note: Area is the primary geometric input shared between S10 and S11

behavior.output: Triggers recomputation of solar gain for that row (m_ROW)
behavior.note: Solar gain = area * SHGC * solar radiation * (1 - shading/100)

behavior.output: Triggers recomputation of winter/summer solar gains
behavior.note: Winter and summer shading applied separately to heating/cooling season gains

behavior.output: Triggers recomputation of utilization factors and utilized gains
behavior.note: Affects how much solar gain is usefully captured vs. overheating

edgeCases.handling: All solar gains = 0, bridged TransmissionLoss areas = 0

edgeCases.rationale: Fully shaded fenestration receives no solar radiation

references.source: TransmissionLossNodes.js (consumes row areas as bridged computed nodes d_88-d_93)
